description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking red and orange color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, with a rounded nose housing a visible piston engine and a two-blade propeller at the front. The cockpit is open and positioned just behind the engine, likely accommodating a single pilot. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers adorned with black cross insignias, connected by a horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear consists of two large floats, indicating this is a seaplane variant designed for water landings. Metal struts and wires reinforce the wings and floats, giving the aircraft a robust, early 20th-century appearance.
description: A vintage red and orange biplane seaplane with twin vertical stabilizers, open cockpit, and large front-mounted propeller. The aircraft features dual floats for water landings and prominent wing struts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, large central float, and the propeller with the open cockpit visible behind.
description_view_1: Rear-side view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers with cross insignias, the tail structure, and the side profile of the floats.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin tail fins, horizontal stabilizer, and the full width of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Opposite rear-side view showing the other side of the tail, wing struts, and the float configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings; no winglets; moderate dihedral angle; strut-braced.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two main floats; fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine; appears to be single-seat with minimal canopy.
